# AI and LLM Agent Instructions for epub2zip

`epub2zip` is a Go **command-line tool**. It extracts the page images from a
fixed-layout EPUB and writes them, in reading order, into a standard ZIP.

> **Source of truth:** the code (`main.go`) and the README. This manual is
> *derived* — if they ever disagree, the code wins.

---

## 1. When to use it (selection surface)

- **Use when:** you have a **fixed-layout** EPUB (illustrated / art / manga book)
  and want its images as an ordered `.zip` — with page order, reading direction
  (LTR/RTL), and spread alignment preserved.
- **Not for:** reflowable/text EPUBs (it detects these and refuses unless `-f`);
  reading EPUB text/metadata generally; or converting arbitrary archives.

## 2. Invocation contract

```
epub2zip [flags] <input.epub> [more.epub ...]
```

- **Inputs:** one or more EPUB paths; `*`/`?` globs are expanded internally
  (handy on Windows shells).
- **Output naming:** with no `-o`, each `book.epub` → `book.zip` in the current
  directory. `-o file.zip` names a single output; `-o dir/` (a directory) writes
  one zip per input into that directory. Multiple inputs with a non-directory
  `-o` is an error (exit 1).
- Unless `-m none`, a `metadata.json` is also written into the zip.
- See the README's **CLI Flags** table for the full list; the agent-relevant ones
  are in §3.

## 3. Unattended / agent use (important)

The defaults assume an interactive human. For scripted or agent runs:

- **Overwrite behavior — `-q` and `-y`.** When the output already exists:
  - with **`-q`** (quiet/unattended): the file is **skipped silently** — no
    prompt, so a quiet run never hangs;
  - without `-q`: the tool prints `Overwrite? [y/N]:` and waits on stdin (a
    non-quiet *unattended* run will hang here — pass `-q`);
  - **`-y`** overwrites unconditionally, in either mode.
  So for agent/unattended runs use **`-q`** (skip existing) or **`-q -y`**
  (overwrite existing).
- **`-d` — dry run (read-only).** Lists the pages it *would* write and creates
  nothing. Use it to preview safely.
- **`-q` — quiet.** Suppresses stdout (see §4) and turns the overwrite prompt
  into a silent skip (above).
- **`-f` — force** processing of a book detected as reflowable (otherwise it
  warns to stderr and skips).

## 4. Output & exit behavior (traps)

- **stdout:** on success the tool prints the output zip path (one per input);
  suppressed by `-q` and by `-d`. **stderr:** warnings and per-file errors
  (`Error processing <file>: ...`).
- **Exit codes** (reliable — use `$?`): `0` = all inputs succeeded; `1` = usage
  error (no input given, or multiple inputs with a non-directory `-o`); `2` = the
  tool ran but **one or more inputs failed** to process (each failure is reported
  on stderr). In a batch, a single bad file yields exit `2` while the others
  still convert.
- **Mutating vs read-only:** a normal run **writes files** (`<name>.zip`, plus
  `metadata.json` unless `-m none`). On an existing output it prompts (interactive),
  **skips** (`-q`), or **overwrites** (`-y`). `-d` is the only fully read-only mode.

## 5. Install

`go install github.com/mixcode/epub2zip@latest` (the `go.mod` module path matches
this), or clone and `go build`. Pre-built binaries are on the GitHub Releases
page — see the README.
